headline: Selangor River Contamination Caused 7 Water Cuts in 18 Months
context: Air Selangor recorded 7 unscheduled water supply disruptions between July 2024 and December 2025 affecting 8.5 million consumers, all caused by upstream pollution incidents.

cards.big: 8.5 million people in the Klang Valley depend on river water.
cards.sub: 7 unscheduled disruptions in 18 months — each lasting 24 to 96 hours — affected hospitals, schools, and businesses.

cards.big: DOE identified 3,200 premises as potential pollution sources in the Klang Valley.
cards.sub: Regular monitoring covers 340 premises — less than 11% of the identified risk pool.

cards.big: Maximum fine for industrial pollution causing water disruption is RM100,000.
cards.sub: A single 48-hour water cut costs consumers and businesses an estimated RM340M.

cards.big: Selangor rejected the Water Services Industry Act 2006 for 17 years.
cards.sub: State control of water means federal pollution standards lack state-level enforcement.

cards.big: When a RM100,000 fine causes RM340M in damage, the fine is not a deterrent. It is a licence.
Water security in the Klang Valley requires penalties proportional to damage, upstream monitoring with real-time enforcement, and federal-state cooperation on the water industry framework.
